This pass read all ten source `.eml` files (page_631–page_640, Yahoo msgnum 633–642, 14–17 August 2002, offset +2 holding steady from batch 63) and reconciled rather than re-drafted: **nine of the ten pages' content was already correctly present** — the "Sail" thread (Mike Westfield's "make it yourself," Ron Eike's three sailmaker names — Dabbler, Douglas Fowler, Todd Bradshaw — and his follow-up, Glen Maxwell's decline-to-recommend and Messing About in Boats anecdote, Alastair's yard-fastening question and Glen's grommets answer) correctly cited in [rig and sail](/design/rig.md) citations 17–23; Dave Bolduc's "Paradox rudder" essay (why the rudder was built large, the Sven Yrvind/Bris comparison, the Little Cruiser rudder tracing and measured offsets) and his offsets-delivery follow-up correctly cited in [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) citations 11–12; and Glen Maxwell's "New Files" interior/chart-table photo announcement present in [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md) — though there mislabeled with a placeholder citation number **`[X1]`** instead of a proper sequential number, now **fixed to `[20]`** and moved to its correct chronological position at the end of the citations list. **Page 640 — "To seal or not?"** (David Beard, redoing "Whisper"'s interior, asking whether to epoxy-seal over the bulkhead-#3 area previously rot-treated per Dave Carnell) was in fact already present in [checking a used Paradox for rot](/construction/rot-inspection-ice-pick.md) (citation 14, the "five-months-later coda" section) from the earlier incomplete run, but had **not been cross-linked** from the boat/person pages a normal batch would touch. Filled those gaps: added a "To seal or not?" section + citation to [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (citation 34) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 100), a short cross-reference addition to [Dave Carnell's boat-building articles](/references/dave-carnell-articles.md), and updated the stale index descriptions in [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (rot-inspection entry), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Whisper/David Beard entries) to mention the August coda — none of which had been updated by the incomplete run. No content was lost or duplicated; no new concepts, promotions, or renames were needed — this batch's one new thread (the rot-reseal question) slots into the already-promoted `rot-inspection-ice-pick.md` leaf. Resolved the "RECONCILE batch 64" and contributed to the "verify log.md integrity" backlog items (see `.okf-progress/backlog.md`); cursor advanced to **640**.
* **New source — Dave's Wiki (aiki.pbworks.com, Dave Raftery), two pages + images**: On user request, scraped two pages from the PBworks wiki of **Dave Raftery** (a prospective Enigma builder; distinct from Dave Bolduc) — "**Advantages of Matt Layden mini cruisers**" and "**Enigma sailboat**" — and **added their contents to the bundle**. Both pages, plus **all 30 wiki-hosted images** (Paradox/Enigma photos; ~2.2 MB), were fetched via curl and **mirrored to `source-web/daves-wiki/`** with a `MANIFEST.tsv`. Added a new source concept, [Dave's Wiki](/references/daves-wiki.md), with citation form `Dave's Wiki (aiki.pbworks.com) — "Page Title" (URL), Dave Raftery — <detail>`; it is treated as a **secondary/compiling source** (lower authority than microcruising.com), valued mainly for the **primary quotations** it carries (Matt Layden c. 2006, George Van Sickle, Sven Yrvind, Don Elliott, "Al"/Alastair, the Bolducs) and for detailed Enigma technical data; pages dated ~2010 and ~2012 (PBworks edit stamps), content 2005–2012. **Chiefly a major enrichment of [Enigma](/matt-layden/enigma.md)**: added fuller Enigma 360 particulars and a construction schedule, and — new to the bundle — the **Enigma 460**, a 2007 4.6 m plywood-hardtop-cabin variant (LOA 15'1", beam 3'9", draft 7⅜", disp 1,010 lb, SA ~95 sq ft, three watertight compartments) whose prototype was built by **George Van Sickle** (sea trials summer 2010), with its own construction schedule and ballast; plus a **Paradox / Enigma-360 / Enigma-460 comparison table**, the light-displacement "crew-weight-as-ballast … could even plane" contrast, a **chine-runner evolution** note ("higher-aspect foils … an improvement over Paradox's runners"), and Matt Layden's own **April 2006 statements that Enigma building plans were being prepared** (relayed also via Don Elliott). **Also enriched**: [chine runners](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md) (the wiki's airfoil/heel/big-rudder definition; the Enigma higher-aspect-foil evolution; and Sven Yrvind's claim that Matt created the chine-runner "in 1982"); [weight and ballast](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (ballast-material densities sand/steel/lead, and Enigma's sand/steel/lead ballast choices); [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) (usable-beam geometry — Paradox 4' minus side shelves ≈ 3'6" vs. Enigma 460's 3'9"; 7'7" crew compartment; sleeps-one/two-snug; George Van Sickle's headroom quote); [furling system](/design/furling-system.md) (a clear third-source description of the boom-rotating drum mechanism); and [sailing characteristics](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md) (sailing/reading standing in the hatch, with Alastair's quote). **Discrepancies flagged rather than smoothed**: the Enigma runner shape is "shorter and **deeper**/higher-aspect" here vs. microcruising.com's "shorter and **wider**"; the chine-runner concept dated to **1982** (Sven) vs. the 1985 first-boat Swamp Thing; and Enigma 360 length given as **12'3"** here vs. 11'10"/3.6 m on microcruising.com. **George Van Sickle** (Enigma 460 prototype builder) and the Paradox "**Enuf**" (a hull with a lift-out rear window) are kept as inline mentions per the people/boats thresholds. Updated the [matt-layden](/matt-layden/index.md) (Enigma entry) and [references](/references/index.md) indexes; no pruning or renames.
* **New source — microcruising.com (David & Mindy Bolduc's website), full site pass**: On user request ("plow the contents of https://www.microcruising.com/ … read page by page in a subagent as we would read the forum contents … add to the okf bundle"), ingested the **entire microcruising.com website** — the first **non-mailing-list** source added to the bundle. The live site (a static site frozen ~Dec 2019) was **crawled and mirrored locally** to `source-web/microcruising/` via a curl-based BFS crawler (WebFetch was 403-blocked; curl worked), then converted to clean text for reading. A **follow-up image scrape** then downloaded **all 1,643 referenced images** (inline photos, gallery thumbnails and the full-size photos they link to, and design-particular diagrams — ~57 MB), preserving the site's directory layout; `MANIFEST.tsv` was regenerated as a full file→URL map (`relpath / kind / bytes / url`) covering all **135 pages + 1,643 images = 1,778 files**. **Reading was done page-by-page by seven parallel Sonnet subagents** (batches A–G: Layden boats; long-form articles; Bolduc's guide/forum commentary; projects/how-tos; the multi-part refit + inventory; trips/blog/news; reviews/famous-boats/misc), each producing a structured findings report (verbatim quotes + suggested destinations) under `scratchpad/mc/findings/`; **integration into the tree was done by the main agent**, with well-scoped new-page drafting and append-only enrichment further parallelized across Sonnet subagents. Established a **website citation scheme** — `microcruising.com — "Page Title" (URL), David & Mindy Bolduc — <detail/quote>` — anchored by a new site-level reference concept. Because the site is authored first-person by Dave Bolduc (the plans seller, who knew Matt Layden personally, owns Little Cruiser, and later Enigma), it is treated as a high-quality **primary source**; opinions are attributed, and content is dated/past-tense per the historical-archive convention. **12 new concepts created**: four Matt Layden **designs** filed under `matt-layden/` (not `boats/`, per the designer-branch rule) — [Terrapin](/matt-layden/terrapin.md) (c.1985, the Maine-knockdown origin of chine runners), [Enigma](/matt-layden/enigma.md) (2005, "Enigma 360"), [Sand Flea](/matt-layden/sand-flea.md) (8' pram), [Elusion](/matt-layden/elusion.md) (9', stand-up-paddle auxiliary); a new person [Mindy Bolduc](/people/bolduc/mindy.md); four new operations/construction leaves — [Gulf Stream crossing](/operations/gulf-stream-crossing.md), [provisioning — food and water](/operations/provisioning-and-water.md), [sculling and yuloh technique](/operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md), [sailmaking](/construction/sailmaking.md), [folding dinghy](/construction/folding-dinghy.md); and two new references — [microcruising.com](/references/microcruising-website.md) and the book [Beach Cruising and Coastal Camping](/references/beachcruising-book.md) (Ida Little & Michael Walsh, which features Swamp Thing). **The whole `matt-layden/` branch was substantially enriched**: [biography](/matt-layden/biography.md) gained a full **design-lineage section** (Terrapin→Swamp Thing→GJAC→Little Cruiser→[20' Balboa interlude with future wife **Karen**]→Paradox→Enigma→Sand Flea→Elusion, with dates, wintering-aboard, Watertribe field-testing, and Matt's own 2019 Cedar Key talk with author Ida Little present); [original Paradox](/boats/original-paradox.md) gained **canonical design particulars** and a cruising record (built 1993; CT→ICW→Ragged Islands; the 1994 tandem-with-Little-Cruiser Bahamas cruise; ~3,000 mi; **the 300-mile Everglades Challenge win now dated to 2003**); [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) gained particulars, the **Gulf Stream freighter-wake rollover-and-self-right** anecdote, and the **Dave Gatan → 2004 hurricane rescue → 2005–2008 rebuild** provenance; [GJAC](/matt-layden/gjac.md) gained its 14'10" lineage and the correction that **its leeboards were added by a later owner** (as designed she used chine runners); [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) gained particulars, a 10,000-mile cruising record, scantlings, the 2005 epoxy/glass sheathing refit, and its sail history. **Design pages enriched**: [chine runners](/chine-runners/origin-and-evolution.md) (origin, "hydrodynamic fence," runner-vs-centerboard boat map and firsthand verdict), [weight and ballast](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (the designer's **layered ballast** — water tanks + stores + lead, with a claimed 165° self-righting), [blue-water suitability](/design/blue-water-suitability.md) (Dave Bolduc's firsthand "**tough coastal cruisers, not offshore boats**" verdict, which largely settles the old February-2002 debate and answers its open knockdown question), plus [rig](/design/rig.md), [rudder](/design/rudder.md), [furling](/design/furling-system.md), and [yuloh](/design/yuloh-and-stowed-rig-retention.md). **Operations enriched**: ground-tackle, heavy-weather-tactics, sailing-characteristics, hull-speed, cabin-heating, auxiliary-outboard-power, trailering, crew-size-and-fit, first-boat-suitability, and group-cruise-reports (the Bolducs' 1993–2013 voyage log, the 1994 buddy-boat cruise with Matt, and the Cedar Key/WCTSS gatherings). **Construction enriched**: bottom-painting, topside-painting, non-skid-deck, epoxy-coating-technique, hull-planking, fastening-and-gluing (paint/epoxy/rot-repair/glass-schedule specs from the Bolduc refits). **People/references enriched**: [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (origin story, Enigma ownership, 2013 solo Bahamas crossing), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) (**confirmed deceased** — "the late Don Elliott" — and his 16-article compilation corroborated), [Chris Morejohn](/people/chris-morejohn.md) (the Hogfish sharpies), and the plans/articles/study-plans references. **Key reconciliations/corrections**: an initial subagent over-correction ("ballast is water, not lead") was caught and integrated correctly as a **layered lead + water + stores** scheme (the SCA interview explicitly gives "several hundred pounds of lead"); the Sketch1.htm "16 part construction article" **corroborates** (does not contradict) the bundle's existing 16-article-compilation vs. 5-part-MAIB-serialization finding; the `physics.bgsu.edu/~layden/…` construction-photo link was **left flagged as unverified** (no university affiliation asserted); minor per-source spec discrepancies (e.g. Enigma beam 43" vs 3'4"; the "sixth micro-cruiser / five during the full-time decade" nuance) were preserved rather than smoothed over. Updated the `matt-layden`, `boats`, `design`, `operations`, `construction`, `people`, and `references` indexes. A bundle-wide internal-link integrity check (2,856 links) confirmed **this pass introduced zero broken links**; also fixed one **pre-existing** broken link surfaced by the check ([plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md)'s `/non-skid-deck.md` → `/construction/non-skid-deck.md`). No pruning or renames; all new Layden boats slot into the already-promoted designer branch, and all site material extends already-promoted, trunk-adjacent concepts rather than fragmenting the tree. The local `source-web/microcruising/` mirror makes every website citation reproducible even if the live site changes.
* **Promotion (structural, no new source read)**: On user request, promoted **GJAC** — one of Matt Layden's older boats, previously an inline mention only (one Sept-2001 rudder photo, one June-2002 resale listing, judged below the boats threshold) — to its own concept at [GJAC](/matt-layden/gjac.md). Filed under `matt-layden/` as a distinct older Layden **design** (a sibling of [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) and [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md)), **not** a `boats/` Paradox hull, since GJAC is a different design (14.5 ft x 4 ft x 9 in, no cabin top, chine runners) rather than a build of the Paradox design. The new concept consolidates both existing data points (the half-immersed rudder photo from page_060 and the full "GJAC is for sail" listing from page_513) with owner Don Musante, dimensions, the no-cabin-top difference, and Dave Bolduc's leeboards-vs-ballast opinion. Added GJAC to [matt-layden/index.md](/matt-layden/index.md)'s Designs list; rewired the inline mentions in [operations/secondhand-market.md](/operations/secondhand-market.md) (now the canonical home for the market/listing side only, its "remains an inline mention" note softened), [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md), and [design/rudder.md](/design/rudder.md) to link to the new concept. No facts or citations lost; no other renames or pruning. Also introduced a new process file, `.okf-progress/backlog.md`, so deferred "promote/consolidate later if X surfaces" hints (like this one originally was) have a durable home that batch workers read and append to.
* **Update (batch page_621–page_630)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 622–630 and 632, 8–14 August 2002, offset +1 confirmed for page_621→622 through page_629→630). All ten confirmed genuinely distinct by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates. **One numbering gap confirmed**: page_630 is msgnum **632**, not 631 — checked `source/page_631.eml` directly (outside this batch's scope) and confirmed it is msgnum **633** ("Re: Sail," a reply to this batch's final message, from `n6pfk`), so the file-to-msgnum offset of +1/+2 continues consistently and msgnum 631 is simply a page genuinely missing from the archive's `page_NNN.eml` capture (not a mislabeling or a page owed to this batch) — flagged for awareness only, no action needed. Three threads/clusters: (1) **tail of "rudder or not"** (page_621, msgnum 622) — David Beard's one-line thank-you to Dave Bolduc's rudder-tracing offer from the prior batch ("Dave, The measurements would be great. thanks"), followed two days later (page_625, msgnum 626) by Dave Bolduc (posting as `mbolduc`/"boldav38") confirming "I'll see if I can measure the rudder up in the next few days" — still no report of an actual measurement. (2) **tail of "Reefing System" / "Reefing / Topping Lift"** (page_622–624 and page_629, msgnum 623–625 and 630) — Bjørn ("bjharbo") thanked Glen Maxwell for the newly posted topping-lift photos; Glen and David Beard then resolved the Lake Weiss location question left open at the end of the prior batch (Glen: "the big lake in Alabama north west of Atlanta... a town on the lake is Cedar Bluff"; David: "about 3 hours away... I will met you there" given notice) without confirming an actual joint sail; three days later Glen posted a follow-up ("More topping lift pictures") confirming he'd completed the internal topping-lift reroute over the weekend, rigged the boat, confirmed "everything worked," and posted further photos to the same "Topping Lift" folder. (3) **new thread "New here, hello all"** (page_626–628, msgnum 627–629) — newcomer **Ricky Idlett** ("thirstydragon2001"), a steamboat-industry veteran (6+ years on the *Delta Queen*/*American Queen*) mid-build on a Stevenson Projects "Triad" kit boat and new to sailing entirely, asked the archive's **twelfth recorded recurrence** of the "where do I get plans?" FAQ, adding unusually rich newcomer biographical color and a request to connect with builders/sailors near Owensboro, KY; Glen Maxwell answered with the familiar David Bolduc contact but, notably, **for the first time in this FAQ's many recurrences, pointed to `http://www.microcruising.com/`** rather than the older `home.triad.rr.com/lcruise` address — an unconfirmed but plausible sign Dave Bolduc's site had moved/expanded; David Beard replied separately the next day with a purely social welcome (sails "in Tenn. a lot," asked how close Ricky was to Kentucky Lake) — no reply from Ricky is recorded. A tenth, standalone page, **page_630 (msgnum 632)**, opened a new thread, "Sail": David Beard reported it was "time for a new sail for Whisper" and asked for a sailmaker who "knows lug sails" — an echo, almost exactly ten months later, of his original October 2001 sailmaker question (Glen Maxwell's "Sails etc." / St. Augustine, FL data point) — unanswered in this batch. **Extended** (no new concept files — all material fit existing, well-matched homes): [design/rudder.md](/design/rudder.md) (rudder-thanks and measurement-confirmed sections, citations 9–10), [design/furling-system.md](/design/furling-system.md) (bjharbo's thanks, the Lake Weiss location resolved, and the 11 August follow-up photos, citations 25–28), [design/rig.md](/design/rig.md) (new sailmaker-request section, citation 16), [references/building-plans.md](/references/building-plans.md) (new twelfth-recurrence section covering Ricky Idlett's introduction and the first `microcruising.com` citation, citations 19–21), [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md) (new-sail section, citation 33), [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (four new dated sections, citations 96–99), [people/glen-maxwell.md](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (two new dated sections, citations 112–114), and [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (extended existing rudder-offer section, citation 29). Ricky Idlett ("thirstydragon2001") stays a one-off inline mention (single message, no boat, no follow-up) per the people threshold, cross-linked from `references/building-plans.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, and `people/glen-maxwell.md`; Bjørn ("bjharbo") likewise remains inline, already tracked from the prior batch. No promotions, renames, or pruning — this batch's material slots cleanly into already-promoted, trunk-adjacent concepts (`rudder.md`, `furling-system.md`, `building-plans.md`, `rig.md`, `whisper.md`). Updated index descriptions in [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (furling system, rudder, and rig entries), [references/index.md](/references/index.md) (building-plans entry, now "at least twelve times"), [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, Dave Bolduc, David Beard entries), and [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (Whisper entry); `operations/index.md` and `construction/index.md` required no changes for this batch.
* **Update (batch page_611–page_620)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 612–621, 5–8 August 2002, offset +1 confirmed: page_611→612 through page_620→621). All ten confirmed genuinely distinct by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps. Two threads: (1) **"2people on board"** (page_611–613, msgnum 612–614) — the tail of Nils's 5 August question (opened at page_609/msgnum 610, already logged in the prior batch as unanswered), drawing three joking, non-substantive replies: David Beard ("You had better know them really well.(Girl friend, Wife)"), Glen Maxwell ("I'll second that, David"), and David LeBlanc (a Lovebird named "Rover"; signed "Seattle, WA USA") — the actual space/logistics question remains unanswered. (2) **"Reefing System" / "Reefing / Topping Lift"** (page_614–618 and page_620, msgnum 615–619 and 621) — David Beard confirms Glen Maxwell's topping-lift fix "works great," one-off poster "bjharbo" asks for pictures, Glen posts the drawing and photos to a new **"Topping Lift"** folder in the files section (noting the message-search terms "Reefing" and "Furling" also surface relevant history), and Glen casually invites David to sail "Zoë" up to Lake Weiss (Alabama/Georgia border) — David can't place it on a map and says he's free for an extended weekend after that week; no resolution recorded. A single interleaved page, **page_619 (msgnum 620)**, is Dave Bolduc's reply in the separate "rudder or not" thread (opened at page_608/msgnum 609 in the prior batch): he agrees a smaller rudder would help Paradox tack quicker and offers to measure/trace Little Cruiser's rudder for David to experiment with — unconfirmed follow-through. **Checked first for pre-drafted content** (per the prior batch's note that a past session over-read to msgnum 611/page_610): confirmed via grep that the prior session's drafting stopped exactly at msgnum 611 (Chris Becker's "Re: Australia," already cited) — none of this batch's msgnum 612–621 content was pre-drafted, so this was a normal new-drafting pass, not a verification/citation-fix pass. **Extended** (no new concept files — all material fit existing, well-matched homes): [design/furling-system.md](/design/furling-system.md) (new "Kudos Glen" topping-lift-confirmed section and Lake Weiss invitation, citations 19–24), [design/rudder.md](/design/rudder.md) (new Dave Bolduc rudder-tracing-offer section, citation 8), [operations/crew-size-and-fit.md](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) (updated the previously-"unanswered" 2people-on-board entry to record the three joke replies, citations 11–13), [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (two new dated sections, citations 93–95), [people/glen-maxwell.md](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (two new dated sections, citations 108–111), [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (one new dated section, citation 28), and [people/david-leblanc.md](/people/david-leblanc.md) (one new dated section, citation 29). "bjharbo" stays a one-off inline mention (single low-content message, no boat) per the people threshold. No promotions, renames, or pruning — this batch's two threads slot cleanly into already-promoted, trunk-adjacent concepts (`furling-system.md`, `rudder.md`, `crew-size-and-fit.md`). Updated index descriptions in [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, Dave Bolduc, David Beard, David LeBlanc entries), [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (furling system and rudder entries), and [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) (crew-size-and-fit entry) to reflect the new content; `boats/index.md`, `construction/index.md`, and `references/index.md` required no changes for this batch.
* **Update (batch page_601–page_610)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 602–611, 30 July – 5 August 2002, offset +1 confirmed; all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps), continuing the "Seamanship" heavy-weather thread from the prior batch, a ninth/tenth "where do I get plans?" FAQ recurrence, a new "construction time" thread, a new "rudder or not" report, a new "2people on board" question, and Chris Becker's long-awaited "Re: Australia" reply. **This batch's content had already been substantively written into the bundle** (evidently during the same work session that produced the prior "page_591–page_600" log entry, which stopped its own citations at msgnum 601 but whose edits to `heavy-weather-tactics.md`, `david-beard.md`, `whisper.md`, `glen-maxwell.md`, `rudder.md`, `build-effort.md`, `crew-size-and-fit.md`, `building-plans.md`, `chris-becker.md`, and `walden.md` already carried the fuller "Re: Seamanship" reply, the plans-FAQ ninth/tenth recurrences, the "construction time" exchange, the "rudder or not" report, the "2people on board" question, and the "Re: Australia" reply through msgnum 611) — so this pass consisted of **verification against the source `.eml` files plus a citation-accuracy fix**, not new drafting. Read all ten source files directly (Python/`email` module unavailable in this environment; used the Read tool on the raw `.eml`s) and cross-checked every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/subject/body against the citations already in the bundle. **Found and fixed a page-number mislabeling bug**: the "Re: Seamanship" reply (lying a-hull, sea anchor, drogue/Chris Morejohn anecdote) is actually **page_605** (msgnum 606), but was cited throughout as "page_606 (msgnum 607)" — the true page_606/msgnum 607 is the unrelated "construction time" question from Nils. Similarly, the "rudder or not" report (Whisper apparently sails better with the rudder up) is actually **page_608** (msgnum 609), but was cited throughout as "page_609 (msgnum 610)" — the true page_609/msgnum 610 is the unrelated "2people on board" question, also from Nils. Corrected all six affected citation lines in [heavy-weather tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (two lines), [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md), and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) to the correct page/msgnum pairs; verified via full-bundle grep that no other page_605–page_610 citation was affected and that page_602/603/604 (the "where can I get plans" mini-thread: Pete/`widge292` asking, David Beard's and Alastair's replies both pointing to Dave and Mindy Bolduc) and page_610 (Chris Becker's "Re: Australia," confirming his identity as "our buddy in Australia" after a house-move absence) were already correctly cited. **Also fixed a stale index description**: [references/index.md](/references/index.md)'s [building plans](/references/building-plans.md) entry said "at least eight times through June 2002," but the concept file itself documents an eleventh recurrence through 2 August 2002 (Nils's combined plans-and-build-time question) — updated to match. No new person or boat concepts were needed — Pete (`widge292`) remains a one-off inline mention per the people threshold, and Nils (`pihlbladnils`/"nils19602002") remains inline (two messages so far, both questions, no boat yet). No renames; no pruning — this was a correction/verification pass over already-drafted content rather than new fragmentation or promotion. Updated [references/index.md](/references/index.md); spot-checked [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), and [design/index.md](/design/index.md) descriptions and found them already accurate and current for this content, requiring no further edits.

## 2026-07-01
* **Update (batch page_591–page_600)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 592–601, 25–30 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps), closing out the "Chine log vs. Taped seam" thread from the prior batch, covering the tail of the "Trailer"/Cedar Key thread, two "Paradox virtual remodelling" Photoshop threads, and a new "Seamanship" thread. **Extended [chine log vs. taped-seam construction](/construction/chine-log-vs-taped-seam.md)**: newcomer benoitbeauchemin thanked Alastair and resolved to "trust the plans"; days later recurring one-off poster **Larry Whited** (`lwhited`, already tracked for introducing the Cedar Key meet) independently corroborated Alastair's structural skepticism of taped-seam construction from his own completed (non-Paradox) **Bolger Micro** build — perpendicular-chine sharpies suit chine logs, not fill-and-tape. **New concept** [Heavy-weather tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md): David Beard opened the archive's first dedicated storm-handling discussion ("Seamanship"), asking the best rudder position while lying to a parachute (sea) anchor; [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) answered that the minimum-force position is centered but hard to hold immobile by rope alone, so pulling the rudder blade up is his practical recommendation, and warned explicitly against lashing it to one side (risks a turning moment that broaches the boat broadside to the waves and risks a knockdown) — directly correcting the "pulled up and to one side" option David had floated. Two days later David asked whether **lying a-hull** is possible under the lug rig (untried, unanswered) and what "Whisper"'s **maximum safe heel angle** is, having sailed her with her deck nearly awash and finding it "felt pretty solid" in flat water — both open questions, cross-linked into [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) and [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md). **Extended [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md)**: Benoit posted two Photoshop "virtual remodelling" images of Paradox (based on a Whisper photo) reshaping the stern against following waves, alongside a status update — he and friends planned a genuine, unmodified Paradox build that winter near Montreal, Quebec — and floated wanting a **20 ft** enlarged Paradox for longer passages, citing an unconfirmed secondhand claim that Dave and Mindy Bolduc have "a first draft of a 23 foot Paradox" on their website; David Beard replied "I really like the Idea of a 20 ft Paradox!!" — the archive's first record of a 23 ft enlargement concept (unverified) and of visual (rather than purely dimensional) stretch speculation. **Extended [group cruise planning and firsthand trip reports](/operations/group-cruise-reports.md)**, **[Zoë](/boats/zoe.md)**, **[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)**, **[David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)**, and **[Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)**: replying to Larry Whited's Cedar Key invitation, Glen independently confirmed he'd meant to attend that year with "Zoë" but forgot, recalled Zoë's long-promised sea trials had run from **Cedar Key to Clearwater**, and revealed he and David Beard were arranging to sail "Whisper" and "Zoë" together for a side-by-side comparison — the archive's first proposed direct hull-to-hull sailing comparison. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — Larry Whited remains an inline mention per the people threshold (recurring but still thin relative to promoted builders). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted construction/design/operations/boats/people leaves or adds one clearly-scoped new operations leaf (heavy-weather tactics, a genuinely new storm-seamanship theme distinct from the existing day-to-day tacking/trim material), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (chine-log-vs-taped-seam, stretching/enlargement, Cedar Key/buddy-boat sailing) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (via variants/index.md), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md).
* **Update (batch page_581–page_590)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 582–591, 15–24 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps), covering a one-line coda to the prior batch's wire-maintenance thread, a major unprompted trim/tacking essay from Dave Bolduc, a newcomer's lugsail-tacking-mechanics question, a Cedar Key small-boat-meet announcement folded into the ongoing "Trailer" thread, a one-off "have you heard from our Australian buddy?" query, and a full "Chine log vs. Taped seam" construction thread. **New concept** [Chine log vs. taped-seam construction](/construction/chine-log-vs-taped-seam.md): newcomer **benoitbeauchemin** asked whether taped-seam plywood construction would be easier than the plans' chine-log method for a total-newbie build; [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), by now halfway through his own hull, gave a detailed defense of the plans' bulkheads-then-sides-then-bottom-then-deck sequence as already easy and well thought out, doubted taped seam was worth the effort, and suspected it would be less rugged given Paradox's heavy displacement — reaching a similar conclusion to February 2002's separate stitch-and-glue debate from a build-sequence angle rather than a weight/ballast one. **Substantially extended [sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md)** and **[Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md)**: Dave's unprompted "Trim is very important" message generalized trim/tacking behavior across all three Layden designs — active point-of-sail crew/ballast trim on Little Cruiser, a stern outboard's weight hurting performance, and, most notably, a design-level explanation of Paradox's documented tacking difficulty: her wide transom and large rudder trade tacking ease for maximum **directional stability**, a priority Matt Layden emphasized specifically for Paradox, whereas Little Cruiser and (especially, thanks to her chine runners) Swamp Thing tack more easily but wander more — reframing the prior batch's unresolved "tacking"/helm-balance debate as partly a designed-in hull property rather than only a trim problem. Cross-linked into **[Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md)**, partially answering Paul V.'s long-open November 2001 question about what makes her sail best. A follow-up newcomer question ("How does a lugger tack anyway?") drew David Beard's confirmation that the rig stays on one side of the mast permanently and never swings over, plus a tacking-with/without-outboard data point for "Whisper" — extended into **[rig and sail](/design/rig.md)**. **Extended [group cruise planning and firsthand trip reports](/operations/group-cruise-reports.md)**: one-off poster **Larry Whited** (kept inline per the people threshold) introduced the annual **Cedar Key** small-boat meet (first weekend of May) as a second, calendar-dated recurring gathering point alongside the ad hoc Apalachicola Bay meetup, noting David Beard started sailing in Little Cruiser there; David Beard's reply asking whether it's the only such regional event went unanswered. **Extended [Walden (Chris Becker)](/boats/walden.md)**: David Beard's short "Australia" query asking whether anyone had heard from "our buddy in Australia (who is building a Paradox)" is recorded as a plausible-but-unconfirmed reference to Chris Becker, the only Australian builder otherwise known in the archive, whose own updates had gone quiet after a January 2002 military-exercise absence — the identification is explicitly flagged as unconfirmed, per the archive's identity-caution guidance, since Chris is not named in David's message. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — benoitbeauchemin and Larry Whited remain inline mentions per the people threshold (newcomers/one-off posters with no boat of their own yet recorded). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted operations/design/construction/people/boats leaves or adds one small, clearly-scoped new construction leaf (chine log vs. taped seam), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (tacking/helm-balance, rig mechanics, plans-vs-alternative-technique debates) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), and [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md).
* **Update (batch page_571–page_580)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 572–581, 9–13 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps; page-number-to-msgnum offset of +1 continues), covering the full reply chain of the "tacking" thread opened in the prior batch and a new, short "Wire maintenance in Mast" thread. **Substantially extended [sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md)**, **[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)**, and **[David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)**: David's tacking report drew technique suggestions from Glen Maxwell (backwind the main; fall off before tacking; reverse out of irons) and David LeBlanc (fall off; check for weight too far forward), David's own full loaded-weight breakdown (400 lb lead ballast, 65 lb outboard, 190 lb himself, fuel/water/gear, ~750 lb total) and confirmation "Whisper" was named by a co-worker (not after Hal Roth's boat, as guessed), then escalated into a substantive theoretical debate between recurring-but-still-below-threshold poster **Larry ("LJGeib")** and David LeBlanc over helm balance — whether a stable, weathervane-like head-to-wind boat can tack at all, lee-helm safety in a knockdown, a badminton-shuttlecock analogy, Gavin Atkin's design rules, and Francis Chichester's downwind storm tactics — that closed with David LeBlanc conceding ("I must defer to your experience") without resolving David Beard's original practical question. **Extended [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md)** with this debate as his first Paradox-handling (rather than construction/materials) contribution and clearest technical disagreement with another list member. **Extended [running lights](/operations/running-lights.md)** and **[Alastair](/people/alastair.md)**: recurring one-off poster **roger chan** (`light369369`, kept inline per the people threshold) asked whether epoxying masthead-light wiring into the mast (as Alastair's own LED masthead unit, described in the prior batch, might require) would force cutting the mast open to replace corroded wire; Alastair answered with his own practical solution — an extractable socket at the mast base, a small silicone-sealed exit hole at the top (untested as of writing), and ordinary 5 amp flex left hanging loose inside (not epoxied in) so it can be pulled and replaced without opening the mast. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — Larry ("LJGeib") and roger chan both remain inline mentions per the people threshold (recurring but still individually thin relative to Alastair/David Beard/Glen Maxwell/David LeBlanc's sustained, multi-thread participation). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted operations/boats/people leaves, consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating the recurring tacking/rig-balance and masthead-lighting themes rather than fragmenting them into new leaves. Updated [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) descriptions.
* **Update (batch page_561–page_570)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 562–571, 4–9 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps; page-number-to-msgnum offset of +1 continues), covering the tail of the "Trailer pictures"/Tropicstar cross-post, a new "Max Hull speed" thread, a "Digest Number 5" newcomer question, the tail of the "Trailer" thread, and a new "tacking" thread. **New concept** [Hull speed — the displacement-speed formula and real-world GPS figures](/operations/hull-speed.md): David Beard asked for the hull-speed formula; Glen Maxwell gave the classic 1.3×√(waterline) formula (≈4.76 kt for Paradox's 13'5" waterline) plus his own logged GPS data from a Clearwater, FL passage — 5.9–6.1 kt sustained for two hours and a 6.3 kt maximum broad-reaching, dropping to 4.7 kt close-hauled into the same seas — the archive's first GPS-timestamped confirmation that Paradox routinely exceeds its nominal hull speed. **Extended [sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md)**, **[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)**, and **[David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)**: after a week-long July cruise, David reported no longer struggling to point but real trouble completing tacks without motor assistance (unanswered) and unreliable jibing, plus a drunk jet-skier's collision with "Whisper" at anchor — no injuries, only cosmetic damage, which David took as proof of "the sound design of the hull." **Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)**, **[trailering](/operations/trailering.md)**, and **[crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md)**: Glen gave a lighthearted inflatable-inner-tube seat-cushion tip for David's trip, and separately answered recurring one-off poster Roger's ("roger chan") direct equipment question about his own trailer — no brakes, but bearing buddies fitted. **Extended [Building the Paradox articles](/references/building-the-paradox-articles.md)** and **[Alastair](/people/alastair.md)**: a new one-off poster, "CJ" (`cj7_579`, kept inline per the people threshold), re-asked the recurring "where can I get Don's articles?" question by quoting an old Alastair/Longyard exchange; Alastair welcomed him and gave Don Elliott's e-mail address directly. **Extended off-topic cross-posts**: a fourth one-off poster, **Steven Keating** (below the people threshold), separately asked the same "who built Tropic Star?" question already asked by David Beard, Longyard, and "malomac" in the prior batch — his message quotes only the original photo caption rather than Dave Bolduc's already-posted answer, suggesting a message crossing in transit; no reply to Steven is recorded. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — CJ/cj7_579 and Steven Keating both remain inline mentions per the people threshold (single-message newcomers). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted operations/boats/people/reference leaves or adds one small, clearly-scoped new operations leaf (hull speed), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (trailering, tacking/rig-balance, Don Elliott's articles, Tropicstar) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), [references/index.md](/references/index.md), and [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md).
* **Update (batch page_551–page_560)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 552–561, 2–4 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps; page-number-to-msgnum offset of +1 throughout, consistent with recent batches), closing out the "furling system"/block-attachment question left open at the end of the prior batch, closing out the June 2002 "Metal source" bronze thread with a second confirmed buyer, and covering a new "Trailer" thread that drifts into one off-topic boat description. **Substantially extended [furling system](/design/furling-system.md)** and **[Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)**: Glen finally answered David Beard's twice-asked block/attachment question with the archive's fullest single hardware description for the topping-lift/furling rig — a stainless-tang boom-end strap (West Marine part# 129544), Harken #021 dinghy blocks throughout, and the complete line routing from boom end to masthead to furling axle, plus his admission that he went through three boom-end straps before getting the design right; David thanked him, Glen replied "My pleasure, David." **Closed out [lower rudder fitting — bearing material options](/construction/rudder-bearing-materials.md)**: William H. Longyard publicly thanked Glen for the bronze stock, confirming receipt the same day as David Beard's own confirmation — the second of three June 2002 buyers (David Beard, Longyard, Roger) with a confirmed-completed transaction; Roger's remains unconfirmed. **Extended [trailering](/operations/trailering.md)** and **[Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md)**: Dave Bolduc gave a general trailer recommendation for Paradox and Little Cruiser (find a fairly heavy-duty used trailer, since both boats' flat bottoms adapt readily), illustrated by his own under-built 1,400 lb Little Cruiser trailer's ongoing gusset-welding and tire failures, contrasted with **Matt Layden**'s own approach — simply buying "a good used heavy duty powerboat trailer with full-sized wheels for cheap" — a new, small data point on the designer's pragmatic, minimal-effort approach to boat-support infrastructure. **New off-topic material** in off-topic cross-posts: Dave Bolduc's trailer-photo caption ("Trailer's for Tropic Star, Little Cruiser and Paradox") drew questions from David Beard, William Longyard ("Ditto!"), and one-off poster **malomac/"Doc"** (already below the people threshold from prior batches) about an unfamiliar yellow sharpie; Dave Bolduc answered with a full description of **"Tropicstar,"** a friend's (Steve's) power conversion of an old Star-class sailboat (keel mostly cut off, 3/4 in plywood cabin, 8 hp Honda outboard, six weeks cruising the Florida Keys on a 1 ft draft) — not a Paradox or Layden design, filed as off-topic color alongside the concept's three existing thin cross-post entries rather than as a new boats/index.md concept. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — malomac/"Doc" remains an inline mention per the people threshold, and Tropicstar (not a Layden design) does not qualify for [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted design/construction/operations/reference/people leaves, consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (furling hardware, bronze sourcing, trailering) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [design/index.md](/design/index.md), [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [references/index.md](/references/index.md), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md).
* **Update (batch page_541–page_550)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 542–551, 30 June – 2 July 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps), directly continuing the prior batch's "Trailer" thread and closing out the "Metal source" thread from two batches earlier, plus one new speculative thread ("paradox (aluminum)") and a new, unanswered furling-hardware question. **Extended [trailering](/operations/trailering.md)** and **[Alastair](/people/alastair.md)**: Alastair added a third never-built retracting-trailer concept (leaving the tow ball on the car and dropping the trailer's back end to the ground); a one-off poster, **kingfm** (no boat of his own, inline mention), pointed the list to the small "T-2000" trailer from Portage Pal, prompting **Glen Maxwell** to give a priced real-world counter-example — the T-2000 can't carry even an empty Paradox, whereas his own custom 2,500 lb-capacity trailer cost only $525 — reinforcing his recurring overbuild advice. **New concept** [Aluminum Paradox (speculative)](/design/aluminum-construction-idea.md): newcomer **"waltsboat"** (previously seen only asking a plans question, now cross-linked from [building plans](/references/building-plans.md)) asked whether an aluminum Paradox was feasible; Alastair objected on designer-fidelity grounds (a good aluminum boat would be designed for aluminum from scratch, drawing an early-fiberglass-boats analogy) while Glen Maxwell gave a detailed, conditional technical answer (5000-series alloy for the hull, 6061 above the waterline, ~3/16 in plate, target within 10% of the wood hull's weight, a flagged condensation concern) — the archive's only recorded discussion of a non-plywood Paradox, never attempted. **Extended [furling system](/design/furling-system.md)** and **[David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)**: David asked Glen what block/brand he used on his topping-lift system and how it attaches to the boom's aft end, repeating the question the next day — left unanswered in this batch, alongside David's still-unresolved furling trouble from the prior batch. **Closed out [lower rudder fitting — bearing material options](/construction/rudder-bearing-materials.md)**: David Beard confirmed receipt of the bronze rod/bar stock ordered from Glen Maxwell two weeks earlier, the only one of three June 2002 buyers (David Beard, William Longyard, Roger) with a confirmed-completed transaction in this batch. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch beyond the one new design leaf — kingfm and waltsboat remain inline mentions per the people threshold (one-off/thin newcomers). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted operations/design/construction/reference/people leaves or adds one small, clearly-scoped new design leaf (the purely speculative aluminum-construction idea), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (trailering, bronze sourcing, furling) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [design/index.md](/design/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), and [references/building-plans.md](/references/building-plans.md).
* **Update (batch page_531–page_540)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 532–541, 28–30 June 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps), directly continuing the prior batch's "Trailer" and "Plans for building Paradox" threads. **Extended [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md)**: waltsboat's previously-unanswered plans question finally drew two replies — Glen Maxwell pointed to Dave Bolduc with a note of mild irritation that the question is asked so often people are starting to ignore it, and a separate one-off poster, **fredmax56/"Steve"** (kept inline per the people threshold), gave Bolduc's full postal address and the first archive mention that the $35 plans package includes a **build CD**; waltandconnie1963's identical question from the same cluster remains unanswered. **Substantially extended [trailering](/operations/trailering.md), [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md), and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md)**: Glen Maxwell finally answered David Beard's ground-clearance question by comparing it to "Zoë"'s own 15 in clearance (adequate everywhere he'd tried except a doubted low-tide Apalachicola launch) and judged David's rebuild fine for Cedar Key; the next day Alastair — bottom now on — opened his own new "must have/avoid" trailer-design question, prompting a run of low-profile/retracting-trailer ideas: Glen suggested drift-boat-type trailers and independently-sprung torsional axles (plus a Northern Tool & Equipment parts link); a one-off poster **"malomac"/"doc"** (recurring below the people threshold from two prior batches) described a motorcycle trailer's winch-and-torsional-axle lowering mechanism; Glen then recalled a never-built concept of his own — torsion axles mounted inside the hull at bulkhead #3, unbolted and floated away after launch — and Alastair countered with a boat-show memory of an aircraft-undercarriage-style winding retraction system (judged flawed) plus his own never-built idea for a suspension-free launching undercarriage paired with a car-transporter trailer. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — fredmax56/"Steve" and "malomac"/"doc" remain inline mentions per the people threshold (one-off or already-tracked-thin recurring posters). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted operations/boats/people/reference leaves, consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating the recurring trailering and plans-sourcing themes rather than fragmenting them into new leaves. Updated [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), and [references/index.md](/references/index.md) descriptions.
* **Update (batch page_521–page_530)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 522–531, 23–28 June 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates, no numbering gaps in this range), concluding the "Metal source" thread begun in the prior batch and covering two new threads, "Running Lights" and "Trailer," plus two more recurrences of the perennial "where do I get plans?" question. **Extended [lower rudder fitting — bearing material options](/construction/rudder-bearing-materials.md)**, **[William H. Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md)**, and **[Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)**: Glen closed out his June 2002 bronze/pipe stock offer, agreeing to sell metal to William Longyard ($14.30 plus tube/postage) and then to recurring one-off poster **Roger** (`light369369`, kept inline below the people threshold) — his bar stock and last rod stock, leaving only bar stock remaining; all three interested builders (David Beard, Longyard, Roger) accounted for, though no completed sale is confirmed in this batch. **New concept** [Running lights](/operations/running-lights.md): David (`buster38801`) asked why Paradox and Little Cruiser use a single masthead all-around white light instead of red/green sidelights; Glen Maxwell gave a COLREGs-based explanation (under-17ft vessels need only a white light; a lone white light reads as another vessel's stern light, putting the give-way burden on approaching traffic; international rules satisfy inland rules but not vice versa) and suggested adding sidelights too for reckless river traffic; **Alastair** described his own compact (<2 in diameter) LED tricolour/all-round masthead unit, wired with only two conductors via opposite-polarity switching — extended [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) accordingly; a one-off "malomac"/"doc" post (previously seen in the prior batch, still below the people threshold) added an unrelated night-safety tip (carry a bright spotlight for oncoming traffic). **Extended [trailering](/operations/trailering.md)**, **[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)**, and **[David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)**: David reported his trailer rebuild had reached 24 3/4 in of ground clearance and asked Glen whether that suits Gulf Coast boat ramps (pontoon tires could gain another 3 in) — unanswered in this batch but a concrete follow-up data point to Glen's own 14 in benchmark from the prior trailer-specification thread. **Extended [building plans](/references/building-plans.md)**: two more one-off newcomers — recurring poster "waltandconnie1963" (already below threshold from a prior Little Cruiser plans question) and new poster "waltsboat" — both asked where to buy Paradox plans, both unanswered, the sixth and seventh recorded instance of this recurring question. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — Roger/light369369, "malomac"/doc, "waltandconnie1963," and "waltsboat" all remain inline mentions per the people threshold (recurring but thin, or single-message). No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted construction/person/operations/reference leaves or adds one clearly-scoped new operations leaf (running lights), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (bronze sourcing, trailering, plans-sourcing) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [references/index.md](/references/index.md), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) descriptions.
* **Update (batch page_511–page_520)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 511–521; msgnum 514 does not appear in this range — a genuine gap in the archive's own numbering, not a duplicate or omission on this batch's part; all ten messages confirmed genuinely distinct via `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body), covering four short threads: "finished boats," "GJAC is for sail," "Furling system," "little crusier plans," and "Metal source." **New material extending [secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md)** and **[rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md)**: Dave Bolduc listed a second, distinct Layden boat for sale — **"GJAC"** (previously known to the bundle only from a single half-submerged rudder photo) — finally identifying her owner (**Don Musante**, Connecticut), dimensions (14.5 ft x 4 ft x 9 in), and key design difference from Paradox (no cabin top); Dave also gave a rare direct opinion favoring ballast over leeboards for lateral resistance, trusting the chine runners. **Extended [lower rudder fitting — bearing material options](/construction/rudder-bearing-materials.md)**: David Beard finally got the bronze/pipe sourcing information Glen Maxwell had promised back in March 2002 — Quality Pipe Products (bronze/brass schedule-40 pipe, tack/sail/yuloh fittings) and Atlas Metal Sales (bronze rod/bar stock), both with 2001 prices, plus Glen's offer to resell his own leftover stock at cost and a proposed builders' co-op; both David Beard and [William H. Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md) asked to buy stock (no completed sale recorded). David's reply revealed he was still "redoing all the metal parts on `Whisper`" well after the boat's refit and naming were complete — extended [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) accordingly. **Extended [furling system](/design/furling-system.md)**: David Beard, still struggling with his own furling setup, asked Glen for an update; Glen reported his topping-lift fix remained trouble-free and detailed his planned internal-reroute path (deck fitting, shared U-strap double block, vent box, cam cleat) in more depth than his earlier April/May mention. **Extended [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md)**: a one-off poster ("waltandconnie1963," below the people threshold) asked where to get plans for Little Cruiser — unanswered, reconfirming the concept's existing finding that no formal Little Cruiser plans exist. **Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)**: a one-off "doc"/"malomac" query about buying a finished Paradox drew Glen's restated four-boats-sailing, none-for-sale census. No new person or boat concepts were created this batch — GJAC remains an inline mention (one listing message, no buyer or build history, below the [boats threshold](/boats/index.md)) and both one-off posters ("malomac"/doc, "waltandconnie1963") stay as inline citations per the people threshold. No renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted person/construction/design/operations leaves, consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes (bronze sourcing, furling, GJAC/rudder variety) rather than fragmenting them. Updated [design/index.md](/design/index.md), [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) descriptions.
* **Update (batch page_501–page_510)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 501–510, 28–30 May 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates), covering three short, closely related threads that close out the "New Pictures"/trailer conversation and open two new ones. Extended [trailering](/operations/trailering.md), [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md): Alastair agreed on overbuilding trailers and described his plan to adapt an existing inflatable-boat trailer; Glen Maxwell then gave the archive's fullest trailer-build specification (David's original trailer was sized for a ~500 lb boat vs. a Paradox's ~900 lb loaded cruising weight; axle/spring/spindle modifications and fender extensions produce a 14 in loaded height to the boat's bottom, just under 2 ft with draft added) — this ~900 lb figure is shown to be consistent with, not contradicting, Glen's earlier ~410 kg tow-weight figure from January 2002, both being the same loaded weight in different units. David Beard closed the thread with a short, standalone "Thank You!" message crediting the list's help for his first-time build. **New material extending [rig and sail](/design/rig.md)**: "Dan" (`dmatyja2002`, previously tracked in [Southern California sailing and the Catalina Island crossing question](/operations/southern-california-and-catalina-crossing.md)) started a scale balsa model and got confused reading the mast's plan views; Glen Maxwell explained the plans draw long thin objects at two scales at once (compressing length relative to cross-sections), and Alastair gave a detailed breakdown of the three mast views (1:1 half-section at deck, whole-mast side-view section, half-section from aft) — the archive's only recorded guidance on reading these specific drawings. **New material extending [transom deck plate](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md)** (retitled in scope, not in title) and [water tank construction and lining](/construction/water-tank-lining.md): recurring one-off list member Roger (`light369369`/"roger chan") asked whether the water tank fills from outside the deck or inside the cabin; Glen Maxwell described his own deck-fill/drain arrangement (funnel, fill-until-gurgling technique, proud-standing drain to avoid hull staining) plus an incidental hose-siphon draining method. Updated [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md), [design/index.md](/design/index.md), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) descriptions. No new person concepts (Roger/"roger chan" remains a recurring one-off below the people threshold, as established in earlier batches); no renames; no pruning — all new material extends already-promoted trailering/rig/transom-deck-plate/water-tank leaves and person pages, consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes rather than fragmenting them.
* **Restructure**: A structural consolidation pass (no new source material read). (1) Created [design/variants](/design/variants/index.md) and moved [stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md), [enlarging Paradox for a tall/heavy sailor](/design/variants/enlarging-for-a-large-sailor.md), and [Paramour](/design/variants/paramour-design.md) into it, consolidating the enlargement/derivative-design cluster under one sub-branch; updated [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (removed the three from the concept list, added a Subdirectories section) and fixed all 18 inbound links across the bundle (people, construction, operations, and the moved files' own cross-links) to the new `/design/variants/...` paths. (2) Merged three thin, off-topic (non-Paradox) cross-post leaves — "The Aegre", the Bahamas charter cross-post, and the Golant Gaffer cross-post — into a single new concept references/off-topic-cross-posts.md, each as its own `##` section with all content/citations preserved; deleted the three originals, updated [references/index.md](/references/index.md), and fixed 8 inbound links (across david-leblanc.md, glen-maxwell.md, mat-man22.md, general-boatbuilding-links.md) to point to the merged file with section anchors. (3) Strengthened the trunk [paradox.md](/paradox.md)'s "Defining features" section into a tight, fully-linked bullet list of the boat's mature, recurring characteristics (chine-runner windward ability, low internal ballast, lug rig worked from the enclosed helm, yuloh auxiliary propulsion, single-thickness plywood construction, enclosed cabin), grounded entirely in existing bundle content, linking the designer branch and known-boats branch, and framing blue-water capability as an open/debated question rather than a settled claim. No facts or citations were lost; no people concepts renamed; construction/operations left unstructured per scope.
* **Update (batch page_491–page_500)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 491–500, 27–28 May 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates), a single continuous thread ("whisper pictures" → "New Pictures") directly following the page_481–490 batch. No new concepts; all material extends already-promoted [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) leaves, plus [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) and [trailering](/operations/trailering.md). David Beard asked for help posting "Whisper" photos to the Yahoo Groups photo album; Glen Maxwell walked him through it. The next day David posted photos of the **completed refit**, and Glen — who had seen the boat in Steinhatchee before the refit — replied he could "not believe it's the same boat." The photos drew three follow-up questions: **Luke S** (one-off poster, inline citation only) asked about the stern outboard bracket, confirmed as a standard West Marine item; **Pete Hodges** asked whether the cabin height matched the plans, and David (5'11") reported "just enough height" to sit up straight, noting the boat's removable rear window — folded into [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) as a real-boat counterpart to the archive's earlier mockup-based headroom discussion; **Alastair** liked the rear window and asked about the trailer, prompting David's account of a near-failure (bent winch, 4-5 broken gear teeth, discovered via a loose strap on the highway) and his conclusion that "Paradox needs a well built trailer" — folded into [trailering](/operations/trailering.md), retitled "tow weight, vehicle capacity, and trailer adequacy" to reflect the added scope, and cross-linked from Alastair's page. Updated [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md), [people/index.md](/people/index.md), and [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) descriptions. No new person concepts (Luke S is a one-off, single-message poster below the people threshold); no renames; no pruning — this batch consolidates a short, self-contained thread entirely within already-promoted branches, consistent with the thought-tree principle of extending strong existing leaves rather than fragmenting new ones.
* **Update (batch page_481–page_490)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 481–490, 7–12 May 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates), closing out the "Southern California Paradox?" thread and covering two new threads, "Pointing" and a fresh "Building progresses" update. **New concept** [Whisper (David Beard)](/boats/whisper.md): David Beard's long-tracked secondhand Paradox (bought from Bob Archibald, refitted through 2001-2002) crossed the naming/notability threshold for its own boat concept when David revealed her name, consolidating boat-level facts previously scattered across his person page and [secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md); cross-linked from [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md). **New concept** [Pointing and tacking-angle expectations](/operations/pointing-and-tacking-angles.md): David asked how well his boat should point, suspecting his 400 lb ballast; Glen Maxwell gave the archive's only concrete tacking-angle figures (110° footing well vs. 90° pointing but "plodding"), a 10%-speed-per-10°-falloff rule of thumb, and a practical afloat bow-down/level-on-the-floorboards trim check — cross-linked from [chine runners and windward performance](/chine-runners/windward-performance.md) and [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md). **Resolved a long-open thread**: extended [mast interior sealing and anchor-light fit](/construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md) with Glen's first-ever disclosed fix for "Zoë"'s six-month-old anchor-light/inspection-port clearance problem (cutting away inside reinforcement structure) plus practical spar-stowage advice (prefers carrying spars in the truck day-to-day; leaves the inspection port open except before an expected following sea, e.g. a Gulf Stream crossing) — prompted by David Beard's related "mast through a deck plate in the transom" question on "Whisper." Extended [Southern California sailing and the Catalina Island crossing question](/operations/southern-california-and-catalina-crossing.md) with a follow-up: newcomer "Dan" asked how many Paradoxes exist in total (having just ordered plans) and Glen Maxwell gave a fresh, independent May 2002 restatement of his December 2001 four-boats census, with a geographic breakdown (two in Florida, one in Tennessee, Don Elliott believed — unconfirmed — to live in Wisconsin); extended [Don Elliott's Paradox](/boats/elliott-paradox.md) with the tentative Wisconsin location. Extended [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) with a new rocky-beach protection sub-thread: David Beard's rudder-bottom roving fix for rock-beach abrasion, and Pete Hodges's two untested follow-up ideas (a split-copper-pipe rudder leading edge; epoxy-glassed steel hull-bottom straps doubling as extra outside ballast, prompted by Glen's own heavy-loading-to-the-waterline comment). Extended [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) and [hull assembly sequence](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md) with his latest "Building progresses" update: side panels assembled to bulkheads, sheer strakes fitted, and — contrary to Don Elliott's article calling it a solo job — turning the hull over in his 2 ft-clearance garage took two people, plus a temporary wedge-and-glue chine-log fitting technique. Extended [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), and [Pete Hodges](/people/pete-hodges.md) with citations for all of the above. Updated the people/boats/operations/design/construction indexes. No new person concepts (Dan remains a one-off, single-topic poster below the people threshold); no renames; no pruning — one new boat concept promoted from consolidated existing material (Whisper) and one new operations leaf (pointing), both directly extending already-tracked David Beard/Glen Maxwell threads, consistent with the thought-tree principle of promoting recurring, well-sourced material rather than leaving it fragmented across person pages.
* **Update (batch page_471–page_480)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 471–480, 30 April – 7 May 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates), closing out the "CAD pictures" and "furling drawing" threads and covering two new threads: "Battery Placement" and "Southern California Paradox?". Extended [notching the stem for chine logs and sheer clamps](/construction/stem-notching.md) and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), [Bert Vercauteren](/people/bert-vercauteren.md), and [Pete Hodges](/people/pete-hodges.md) with the CAD-gallery thread's coda: Bert congratulated Alastair on the finished 20-picture CATIA gallery and asked how many hours it took; Pete added a one-line thanks; Alastair answered with his professional CAD background (~15 years on IBM CADAM at work, newly learning CATIA, an earlier rejected attempt at AutoCAD at home) rather than an hour count, plus general software-learning advice. Extended [furling system](/design/furling-system.md), [Pete Hodges](/people/pete-hodges.md), and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md): Pete could not relocate a previously posted furling-line sketch; Glen identified it as his own "Zoë" topping-lift drawing (archive message 206) and reported he was now running that line internally alongside the halyard/furling line. **New material** in [interior bins and hinged floor](/design/interior-bins-and-floor.md): Pete Hodges asked how a 65 lb battery is meant to fit through the forward stateroom's ~1 ft opening; Glen Maxwell answered that all interior panels are simply removable, and — answering Pete's follow-up about the forward-cabin wall's purpose — relayed a notable secondhand claim: **Matt Layden reportedly lived aboard his own Paradox for two to three years, cruising from Nova Scotia to the lower Bahamas**, likely explaining why every compartment has a planned use. This is the most specific range/duration given anywhere in the archive for Matt's own cruising and bears directly on the still-open coastal-vs-blue-water question; extended [Matt Layden (biography)](/matt-layden/biography.md) and [blue-water suitability](/design/blue-water-suitability.md) with the claim, flagged throughout as secondhand/unconfirmed per Glen's own "if what I have been told is true" caveat. **New concept** [Southern California sailing and the Catalina Island crossing question](/operations/southern-california-and-catalina-crossing.md): one-off newcomer "Dan" (Garden Grove, CA, below the people threshold) asked whether a Paradox owner sails in Southern California and has crossed to Catalina; Susan Davis ("futabachan") answered that Paradox routinely crosses the Gulf Stream to the Bahamas, "longer and rougher" than a Catalina crossing — cross-linked from blue-water suitability as a second, independent Gulf Stream benchmark. Lightly touched the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md) capability section with the Gulf Stream/Nova-Scotia framing. Updated the design/operations/matt-layden indexes. No new person concepts (Dan is a one-off, single-message poster below the people threshold; Susan Davis remains recurring-but-boatless, kept inline per longstanding precedent in this bundle); no renames; no pruning; no trunk restructuring beyond the capability-section addition — all new material extends already-promoted CAD/furling/interior/blue-water threads or adds one small, clearly-scoped new operations leaf, consistent with the thought-tree principle.
* **Update (batch page_461–page_470)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 461–470, 17–29 April 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates), closing out the "Refit" cruising-tape thread and covering two new threads: a recurring "how do I buy the plans?" newcomer question, and a new "Gluing sheers and Chines" construction thread. Extended [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) with [Alastair](/people/alastair.md)'s one-line addition to the shakedown-cruise-tape interest list ("very interesting to see a paradox on the move"), now three list members (Bjørn, Kevin, Alastair) wanting a copy. **New inline mention** (below the people threshold): new member **Ernie Fossel** (`erniefossel`/`whittlinfool`) asked the now-familiar "where do I buy the plans?" question after ordering Don Elliott's articles — recorded in [building plans](/references/building-plans.md) alongside [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)'s now-fifth-recorded pointer to Dave Bolduc ($35 US) and [Kevin](/operations/first-boat-suitability.md)'s pointer to the [Micro Cruising group](/references/micro-cruising-group.md), which he confirmed Dave Bolduc still moderates. Extended [notching the stem for chine logs and sheer clamps](/construction/stem-notching.md) and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) with the CATIA follow-through promised a month earlier: a finished **20-picture gallery** posted to a dedicated page on Alastair's own website, drawing Glen Maxwell's approval ("Looks like the real thing to me"). **New construction thread**, filed as a new section of [fastening and gluing](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md) rather than a new leaf (directly extends that concept's existing screws-vs-nails/trial-fit material): Alastair asked how builders time the epoxy when bending the sheer strakes and chine logs into position, worried pre-applied glue would go off mid-bend; Glen Maxwell answered that his own (done with slow hardener, one piece at a time, under 2 sq ft each) had no such problem, then corrected Alastair's assumption that this meant glue-then-fit — his actual method is **dry-fit first** (using removable sheet-rock screws to test-fit repeatedly), **then** remove, glue, and refit for good, resolving Alastair's original worry entirely. Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) with citations for all of the above. Updated [people/index.md](/people/index.md) and [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) descriptions for Alastair, fastening-and-gluing, and stem-notching. No new person concepts (Ernie Fossel is a one-off, single-message poster below the people threshold); no renames; no pruning; no trunk promotion — all new material extends already-promoted person/construction/reference leaves (David Beard's refit narrative, the CATIA-model thread, the recurring building-plans question, and the fastening-and-gluing concept), consistent with the thought-tree principle of consolidating recurring themes rather than fragmenting them.
* **Update (batch page_451–page_460)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 449–460, 29 March – 17 April 2002, all genuinely distinct messages confirmed by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body). Three messages (page_451–453, msgnum 449–451) are purely administrative "yahoo derived spam" warning/reply chatter about Yahoo Groups' marketing-preference defaults, with zero Paradox content — skipped, consistent with the archive's existing treatment of non-boat noise. The remaining seven extend two already-tracked builders' timelines rather than opening new threads. Extended [Epoxy brands, chemistry, and cure behavior](/construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md): David Beard ("buster38801"/`dlb`) asked whether a reddish-discolored, roughly year-old West System fast hardener was still good; Glen Maxwell confirmed epoxy simply darkens with age and has an "almost indefinite" shelf life; David's refit update in the same message — bottom on, first coat of bottom paint applied via Glen's bonding method, antifreeze-treated plywood taking almost a month to dry, expected launch in about two weeks — and Glen's same-day confirmation of their previously proposed weekend meetup. Extended [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) further with a two-weeks-later "Refit" update (14 April 2002, accidentally posted twice — page_457/458 are word-for-word identical messages sent under a minute apart by the same author, a genuine double-post rather than an archive-duplication artifact): finishing painting, launch expected the next week, and an offer to tape the shakedown cruise (sailing/cooking/sleeping) to show "what cruising in a Paradox is all about" — drawing interest from one-off poster "bjharbo" ("Bjørn," inline citation only, below the people threshold) and from already-tracked list member [Kevin](/operations/first-boat-suitability.md) (`kevin1b`). Extended [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) with a 13 April 2002 "Building progresses" update: all spars completed and epoxy-coated, side panels scarfed and epoxy-coated, a successful solo trial assembly of the hull (manageable alone, though more clamps would have helped), and all notches cut/holes drilled, with reorganizing his garage's timber stock as the next blocker before final assembly — his most advanced reported build state to date. Updated [people/index.md](/people/index.md) descriptions for David Beard and Alastair to reflect their April 2002 states per the temporal-framing guidance, and the construction index for the epoxy-chemistry concept.
