* **Update (batch page_161–page_170)**: Ten archived pages completing the "A Query" census thread and a new December 2001 "Paint" thread. Extended [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md)'s census with the answers: [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) reported only **four Paradoxes known to be under sail** (Matt's own boat, Don Elliott's, David Beard's, and "Zoë"), naming David Beard, Alastair, Chris Becker, and Jeff Forrest as then-building, and extended an open sailing invitation from his home near Jacksonville, FL; [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) confirmed he had **one Paradox under construction and one in the driveway** (Tupelo, MS); and [Jeff Forrest](/people/jeff-forrest.md) reported **beginning construction** in Southern Oregon using Dave Bolduc's plans, Don Elliott's articles, and already-purchased marine ply. Created new construction concept [Topside and hull painting choices](/construction/topside-painting.md) — David Beard's question about cheaply painting his secondhand Paradox with ordinary house paint, answered by Glen Maxwell's own house-paint/West-Marine-Sea-Gloss experience, his "Zoë" Awlgrip data point (only 1 qt used), and his roll-and-tip (3 thin coats) technique, plus James Fuller's pointer to an external article arguing water-based paint is superior — filed as a sibling to, and distinguished from, the existing [bottom painting over epoxy](/construction/bottom-painting.md) (which addresses antifouling bonding, not finish-paint choice). Created new reference [Dave Carnell's boat-building articles](/references/dave-carnell-articles.md) for that external site. Extended [self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md) with a minor, unresolved external pointer (two Wharram-catamaran-website links posted by one-off contributor "pererieravicens," suggesting a possibly adaptable vane/rudder design — no follow-up recorded). No new person concepts for one-off posters James Fuller, Ron Eike (recorded in a prior batch), or "pererieravicens," per the people-threshold guidance. No renames or pruning this batch; no trunk promotion — all new material is leaf-level detail (a census snapshot, a paint-economy thread, a minor external link) consistent with the thought-tree principle. Updated the people/construction/references indexes.
* **Update (batch page_151–page_160)**: Ten archived pages, mostly continuing December 2001 threads. Created new operations leaf [Newcomer FAQ — cabin comfort, capacity, and perceived safety](/operations/newcomer-faq-comfort-and-safety.md) for a distinct cluster of first-impression questions from non-builder **Kevin** (`kevin1b`) — cabin comfort with the hatch shut, two-person capacity, forward-hatch crawl safety, general seaworthiness, on-deck reefing, and on-deck safety given the boat is "only 4' wide" — left unanswered in this batch and flagged for later reconciliation. Substantially extended [Cabin heating for cold-weather sailing](/operations/cabin-heating.md) with three new data points: a vented-exterior-heat-source link (Zodi "Hot Vent," kingfm), Matt Layden's own anecdote of deliberately freezing his boat into a Cape Cod saltwater pond and relying on body heat plus solar gain (relayed by Dave Bolduc), and Glen Maxwell's inverted-clay-flower-pot heat-retention trick for the cabin stove (with a repeated no-sleeping-with-the-stove-lit warning). Extended [Ground tackle and anchoring](/operations/ground-tackle.md), [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md), and [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) with a reconfirmation that Matt's stern-throw/walk-forward anchoring method is used identically and successfully on Little Cruiser, plus the historical detail that Little Cruiser's Bruce anchor originally just hung off the stern. Extended [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) and [Secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md) with his December 2001 "My Paradox" reply — sailing the ex-Archibald boat ("no Zoë, but strong") while resuming his own build, rejecting Chris Becker's icebox idea for lack of room, and deciding to leave bulkhead #1 solid/watertight — met by [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md)'s caution to keep bulkhead-#1 access for future inspection. Extended [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md), [Walden](/boats/walden.md), and [Build effort and workspace](/construction/build-effort.md) with his "A Query" census thread (naming Alastair, Glen Maxwell, and Don Elliott as known builders/owners, with Ron Eike — one-off, no new person concept — asking to see a boat in person) and a new plywood-cost data point ($AUS790 for all ten sheets of Pacific Maple marine ply). Extended [Wind Vane Self-Steering (book)](/references/wind-vane-self-steering-book.md) with an availability follow-up (out of print by Dec 2001; purchasable via armchairsailor.com for $36.95). No renames, pruning, or trunk promotion this batch — all new material is leaf-level detail or reconfirmation of existing themes, consistent with the thought-tree principle. Updated the operations/people/construction/references/boats indexes.
* **Update (batch page_141–page_150)**: Resolved the previously-open [self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md) question: [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) reported that in light air the Paradox "sails herself" via tiller-rope friction (moved around inside and made a sandwich on an overnighter without the course changing), while ["guylita"](/people/guylita.md) — now recorded across four messages in one day — qualified this by stressing that hands-free tiller capability matters more in stronger winds (to reef, and because an unattended tiller can jibe the boat fast under weather helm). A later message (judecarm2) added a candidate purpose-built solution, creating new reference [Wind Vane Self-Steering (book)](/references/wind-vane-self-steering-book.md) (Bill Belcher's plywood wind-vane plans). Extended [rig and sail](/design/rig.md) and the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md) with a defining, recurring clarification from Glen: **all sail handling including reefing is worked from the helm station, with no need to go forward** — promoted to the trunk as a well-supported theme. Created two new leaf concepts for guylita's further unanswered questions: [Sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md) (weather helm to windward, sail-trim counteraction, heeling) and a new "Ballast placement: outboard vs. centered" section in [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (torque/oscillation concern) — both left open per the archive. Created new operations concept [Cabin heating for cold-weather sailing](/operations/cabin-heating.md): David Beard's question met with a joking Coleman-gas-light suggestion (king/kingfm) and a serious carbon-monoxide safety rebuttal (LJGeib, citing deaths from unvented heaters in enclosed spaces; noting expensive marine coal/pellet heaters exist) — no solution adopted. Extended [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md) and [Walden](/boats/walden.md) with a December 2001 status update: the build had stalled for months (work/health) and Chris resumed, ordering the rest of the ply and epoxy, floating an icebox and detachable-sink idea. No renames or pruning this batch. Updated the operations/people/references indexes and the trunk.
* **Update (batch page_131–page_140)**: Closed out the November 2001 "Stretch Paradox" thread with two more independent questioners — [Pete Hodges](/people/pete-hodges.md) (new person; asked whether mast height/sail area must scale with hull length) and [Jeff Forrest](/people/jeff-forrest.md) (new person; introduced himself as an incoming builder, asked whether beam scales too, then asked whether anyone had actually built a stretched Paradox — left unanswered in this batch) — both answered by Glen Maxwell with the same "scale everything uniformly" rule; extended [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md) and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) accordingly. Extended the "Anchoring" thread from the previous batch: [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) proposed a permanent snap-hook bow line doubling as a safety-harness jackline (worrying it would leave the boat stern-to-wind when weighing anchor), and Glen resolved that by advising builders to raise sail before hauling in the anchor — folded into [ground tackle and anchoring](/operations/ground-tackle.md). Created new construction concept [Removable foam flotation panels (door-skin technique)](/construction/removable-foam-panels.md): Glen's method (worked out months after leaving foam out of "Zoë" over moisture-inspection and cosmetic concerns) for mounting foam on removable, paintable 1/8 in door-skin panels rather than gluing it to the hull — cross-linked as a practical follow-up to the earlier [plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md); Alastair endorsed it and noted it also makes expanded polystyrene a viable, protected flotation material. Created new operations concept [Self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md) and new person stub ["guylita"](/people/guylita.md) (screen name only) for a single unanswered question — whether Paradox can self-steer or needs a wind vane — flagged as an open question for later batches to resolve, per the identity-caution and thought-tree conventions (kept as a lean leaf, not promoted, since it is a single unanswered one-liner). No renames or pruning this batch; no trunk promotion — all new material is single-exchange, leaf-level detail (rig-scaling clarifications, an anchoring refinement, a construction technique, and an open handling question) consistent with the thought-tree principle. Updated the people/construction/operations indexes.
* **Update (batch page_121–page_130)**: Six of the ten archived pages in this batch (page_121–123) are the tail of the JS/Kak virus saga — David Beard's 22 Nov 2001 apology and two "all clear" replies (Ron Eike, and a previously unrecorded list member "Bob") — folded into [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)'s citations with no new person concept for "Bob" (one-off, per the thought-tree principle). The remaining pages continue two genuine threads: (1) the "Mast construction"/"Interior photos"/"More photos posted" exchange (page_124–128) in which [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) asked [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) for a "Zoë" photo with a person aboard for scale, and Glen followed up with scale photos plus four from **"Zoë"'s first overnighter** (full stores and water tanks) — reporting she still needed roughly **100 lb more ballast** to feel stiff enough for confident forward movement on deck; and (2) a new "Anchoring" thread (page_129–130) where [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) flagged the real hazard of having to go forward to the bow to anchor on an enclosed boat, and Glen answered with a **cockpit-worked inhaul-line technique** (flake rode aft to the stern cleat by 7× depth, deploy/set without going forward, retrieve via a separate ring/carabiner inhaul line) that lets the whole anchoring evolution be worked from the cockpit/cabin. Extended [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) with the photo/overnighter narrative; extended [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) with the real-world under-ballasted data point; substantially extended [ground tackle and anchoring](/operations/ground-tackle.md) with the new inhaul-line technique (a good complement to the plans' below-deck-stowage rationale already recorded there) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) accordingly. No renames, pruning, or trunk promotion this batch — the anchoring technique and ballast data point are useful practical leaves, not yet a defining structural fact, consistent with the thought-tree principle. Updated the design/operations index descriptions.
* **Update (batch page_111–page_120)**: Eight of the ten archived pages in this batch (page_111–114, 116–118) continue the same November 2001 JS/Kak (KakWorm) virus outbreak documented in the previous batch — David Beard's outgoing mail kept carrying the virus despite Norton scans finding nothing on his end, reported repeatedly by David Beede, James Fuller, Glen Maxwell, and Ron Eike — recorded only as citations on [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), with no new one-off person concepts (James Fuller, David Beede) per the thought-tree principle. The two substantive threads: (1) confirmation that David Beard successfully collected the secondhand Paradox from Bob Archibald in Florida and went sailing (closing the loop from the previous batch's purchase saga), folded into [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md); and (2) a "Mast construction" thread (page_119–120) where [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), having finished all bulkheads and gone into winter semi-hibernation, asked whether to epoxy-coat the mast interior, and [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) answered yes and disclosed that "Zoë"'s finished mast turned out 1.5 in too long to stow with the inspection port closed because the plans didn't account for the anchor light's height — a cautionary build-fit lesson. Created new construction concept [Mast interior sealing and anchor-light fit](/construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md). Extended [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md) with Glen's trailer-modification detail (lengthened axle atop the spring, welded spindle, low bunk boards enabling launch in under 2 ft of water) posted alongside new interior/trailer photos requested by David Beard. Extended [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) accordingly. No renames, pruning, or trunk promotion this batch — the mast-sealing/fit lesson is a useful but single-exchange construction leaf, consistent with the thought-tree principle. Updated the construction index.
* **Update (batch page_101–page_110)**: Nine of the ten archived pages in this batch (page_101, 102, 104, 105, 107, 108, 110) are pure list-administration noise — a chain of November 2001 messages (David Beard, Les Lampman, Ron Eike, Glen Maxwell) about a JS/Kak e-mail worm circulating via Outlook Express replies, with zero boat content; recorded only as a brief citation aside on [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), with no new person concepts created for one-off participants (Les Lampman, Ron Eike) per the thought-tree principle. The substantive content is a two-message resolution of the previous batch's open "Stretch Paradox" question (page_103, page_106): Glen Maxwell relayed **Matt Layden's own rule of thumb** for lengthening a Paradox — multiply scantlings by the enlargement factor (e.g. 15%), safe up to ~25% beyond which bulkhead placement and scantlings must be rethought from scratch — and recommended Dave Gerr's *The Elements of Boat Strength* as a scantling reference; [Charles Wilson](/people/charles-wilson.md) confirmed the answer matched what he'd suspected. Updated [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md) from an open question to an answered design concept, and extended [Charles Wilson](/people/charles-wilson.md) and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) accordingly. No new concepts, renames, or restructuring this batch — all sourced content was folded into existing concepts; nothing here rises to trunk-level promotion (a single Q&A exchange, not yet a recurring theme). Updated the design index description.
* **Update (batch page_091–page_100)**: Added the continuation and resolution of the November 2001 "Paradox for sale" thread (the Steinhatchee, FL boat owned by Bob Archibald) plus Glen Maxwell's launch of "Zoë." Extended [secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md) with the thread's resolution: [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), after weeks of trying, finally reached the seller (with help from list member **roger chan**, who supplied the owner's e-mail) and announced on 2001-11-17 he was picking up the boat to sail until his own build was finished. Extended [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md) and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) with the boat's **launch on Friday the 13th** and first-sail report (2.2–3.3 kts by GPS in <5 kt wind, only 75 lb ballast aboard, resisted heeling past 45° by hand) plus a minor virus-notification aside. Extended [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) with an unanswered question from new person concept [Paul V. (cruisenewsnet)](/people/paul-v.md) — explicitly distinguished from the earlier, unrelated [Paul (NZ)](/people/paul-nz.md) — asking what design factor (keel? beam? displacement?) makes Swamp Thing sail better than Paradox/Little Cruiser. Extended [Charles Wilson](/people/charles-wilson.md) and created new design concept [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md): the archive's only recorded proposal (unanswered in this batch) to lengthen the Paradox hull to 16 ft, distinct from the previously documented chine/rudder detail iterations. No renames or pruning this batch — all new material is leaf-level detail (an open design question, a resolved sale, a launch report) consistent with the thought-tree principle; no promotion to the trunk was warranted since nothing here changes the boat's defining, well-established facts. Updated the people/design/boats/operations indexes.
* **Update (batch page_081–page_090)**: Added detail from the conclusion of the "Foam flotation follow-up" thread, a new "Sail" thread (Oct 2001), Matt Layden's forwarded chine-runner sea trial, a repeat "plans" query, and a "Paradox for sale" cost-comparison thread (Nov 2001). Extended [plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md) with its dramatic conclusion: [Charles Wilson](/people/charles-wilson.md) (new person concept) defended Don Elliott's credentials, and Don then **resigned from `paradoxbuilders`** ("I will not belong to any group that promotes unsafe practices"), redirecting members to a new reference concept, [Smallboats (Yahoo group)](/references/smallboats-group.md). Created design concept [Chine runners and windward performance](/chine-runners/windward-performance.md) — Matt Layden's own rigorously-documented, quantified sea trial (triangular course, 3 timed circuits, VMG/VMGW data) of a modified port chine runner, proving Paradox sails to windward passably well without a centerboard; cross-linked into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md), [Matt Layden's biography](/matt-layden/biography.md), and [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) as a companion, better-evidenced sibling to the earlier chine-extension experiment. Extended [rig and sail](/design/rig.md) with a sail-sourcing data point (Glen Maxwell's $420 4.5oz Dacron tan-bark sail from Sails etc., St. Augustine FL) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) with his sailmaker query. Extended [build effort and workspace](/construction/build-effort.md) — retitled in scope to cover cost — with new cost figures from Dave Bolduc: Little Cruiser $1,600 (secondhand, needing refit), Paradox ~$2,000/3 months to build, Swamp Thing $600 (secondhand, bought by Dave Gatan); noted an unreconciled 4-months-vs-3-months discrepancy across Dave Bolduc's own messages on how long Matt's original Paradox build took. Extended [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) with Dave Bolduc's enthusiastic endorsement ("the best sailing boat of all of Matt's designs," compared to an Albert Strange canoe yawl) and [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) with its purchase price. Extended [building plans](/references/building-plans.md) with a third recurrence of the plans-sourcing question (Judy Carman) answered by Glen Maxwell and Charles Wilson. No renames this batch; no pruning — all new material is either promoted (chine-runner trial into the trunk) or filed as leaves consistent with the thought-tree principle. Populated the people/references indexes.
* **Update (batch page_071–page_080)**: Added detail from the continuation of the September 2001 "Paradox for sale in Florida" thread and two new October 2001 threads ("Hi" / new-builder introduction, and "Foam flotation follow-up"). Extended [Secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md) with Glen Maxwell's full condition report on the Steinhatchee boat (steel fittings vs. bronze, like-new sail, single layer of bottom cloth, cracked exterior glass, "not a Crown Jewel," Glen's 9-boat fleet) and a real-world shipping-cost data point (Barend Migchelsen: ~C$900 to ship a 14 ft scow Netherlands→Canada by container, 20 years prior). Created construction concept [Plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md): Don Elliott's claim (backed by System Three and West System) that foam flotation is safe over epoxy-coated fir plywood, and Glen Maxwell's rebuttal quoting West System's own manual on Marine-vs-lesser-grade veneer checking risk, disclosing he used "ext A/B" ply on "Zoë" and found the finishing-labor cost too high. Created person concepts [Jeff (UK, "doryman_uk")](/people/jeff-doryman.md) (reacted to the $500 listing, then reported ordering materials for his own Paradox derivative, to build in a lean-to starting spring 2002) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (resumed a paused build — bulkheads done pre-divorce, restarting autumn 2001). Extended [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md), and [Micro Cruising group](/references/micro-cruising-group.md) (noting Glen's cross-post of the plywood rebuttal to both lists) with cross-links to the new material. No restructuring or renames this batch; no promotion to the trunk (the plywood-grade debate and new builder introductions are single-exchange, leaf-level detail per the thought-tree principle). Populated the people/construction/operations indexes.
* **Update (batch page_061–page_070)**: Added detail from the September 2001 "Lead" (ballast installation), "Where can one get the plans and cost?" (repeat), and "Paradox for sale in Florida" threads (10 archived pages, Msgnum 57–66). Extended [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) with a new "Installing the lead ballast" section: Don Elliott's tapped-hardwood-block method for lead fastenings (vs. Glen Maxwell's waxed-machine-screw alternative), the forward-compartment trim lead that counterbalances the battery (nylon-strapped), lead sealed in plastic with rubber padding, and Don's stated intent to write up final building steps for **MAIB** (*Messing About In Boats*) magazine. Extended [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) and [Don Elliott's Paradox](/boats/elliott-paradox.md) with this lead-installation detail, and [building plans](/references/building-plans.md) / [Micro Cruising group](/references/micro-cruising-group.md) with a repeat plans-and-cost query (from new member David Beede) that reconfirms Dave Bolduc as sole authorized plans seller at ~$35. Created operations concept [Secondhand Paradox market](/operations/secondhand-market.md): a rare resale data point — a complete sailing Paradox offered for $500 in Steinhatchee, FL (owner Bob Archibald, a Bolger-boat collector) in September 2001. No restructuring or renames this batch; no new full person-concepts were warranted (David Beede and Bob Archibald are one-off mentions, recorded inline/in citations per the thought-tree principle). Populated the operations index.
* **Update (batch page_051–page_060)**: Added detail from the September 2001 "Another Paradox"/naming, "Pictures of Little Cruiser interior", "Building continues", and "Chine modification info"/"Rudders" threads (10 archived pages covering 10 distinct messages, Msgnum 47–56 — no true duplicates, despite two of the threads reusing similar wording across replies). Created design concept [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) (Matt Layden trial-sailed a forward chine extension on Paradox, found little benefit, and does not recommend it; next considered a "Little Cruiser style" rudder since the balance point reportedly differs; four different Layden rudders photographed, including one boat identified only as "GJAC's") and construction concept [hull assembly sequence](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md) (Don Elliott's stem-first order, tank top/floor doors positioned early, hot-wet-blanket forming of side panels onto the stem, and repeated tack-and-bevel dry-fits before gluing). Extended [Alastair](/people/alastair.md) (finished assembling all bulkheads; garage-size observations; awaiting boards for the stem), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) (hull-assembly coaching; offered a free cutaway drawing of Little Cruiser's interior for Dave Bolduc's book, noting no plans/drawings were ever made for that boat), [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (called Matt Layden "a modern day Thoreau" on hearing Chris Becker's boat would be named "Walden"; posted Little Cruiser interior photos; reported the chine/rudder experiments), [Walden](/boats/walden.md) (the Thoreau naming story), and [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) (no plans exist; cutaway drawing offer; rudder cross-pollination with Paradox). Populated the design and construction indexes; promoted the chine/rudder design-iteration theme and the stem/wet-blanket assembly technique into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md). Did not create a stub concept for "GJAC" (only a rudder photo and boat name are known — noted inline in [rudder and chine experiments](/design/rudder.md) instead, per the thought-tree principle of keeping one-off mentions as leaves rather than new nodes).
* **Restructure**: Reorganized the bundle into designer/design and individual-hull branches. Created top-level [matt-layden](/matt-layden/index.md) (the designer): moved the biography to [/matt-layden/biography.md](/matt-layden/biography.md) (removing `/people/matt-layden.md`, leaving a pointer in the people index) and moved his other designs [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) and [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) out of `references/`. Created top-level [boats](/boats/index.md) for known Paradox hulls — [original Paradox](/boats/original-paradox.md) (Matt's prototype), [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md) (Glen Maxwell), [Walden](/boats/walden.md) (Chris Becker), [Chris Curtis's Paradox](/boats/curtis-paradox.md), and [Don Elliott's Paradox](/boats/elliott-paradox.md). Added `matt-layden` and `boats` to the root index and a "Known boats" pointer to the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md). **Identity correction:** softened the earlier over-assertion that the 2019 groups.io owner "Mattsplatt" (signed "Matt") is designer Matt Layden — now stated as *plausible but unconfirmed, pending archive evidence* (in the [biography](/matt-layden/biography.md) and the [mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md)). Updated all inbound links to the moved files; no sourced facts or citations were lost.
* **Update (batch page_041–page_050)**: Added detail from the July–Sept 2001 "Lugsail", "Changes to plans", "More plan questions", "Another Paradox", and "Sorry I took so long to come aboard" threads. Created person [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (plans distributor, Little Cruiser owner, deep knowledge of Matt's boats) and [Chris Morejohn](/people/chris-morejohn.md) (Exuma Sound helmsman); references [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) (another Layden boat Dave helped restore) and [Micro Cruising (Yahoo group)](/references/micro-cruising-group.md); and design concept [interior bins and hinged floor](/design/interior-bins-and-floor.md) (bin panels between bulkheads #1/#2, battery behind port panel, toggle-hinged floor, plan scales 1:5/1:10). Extended [rig and sail](/design/rig.md) with Glen's authoritative answers (no-parrel is a deliberate stow-alongside-cabin compromise; tack through a brass deck pipe acting as a swivel; sail-foot sleeve over the rolling boom, tied fore/aft; Exuma Sound 25 kts / 6 kts photo), [ground tackle](/operations/ground-tackle.md) (anchors-as-ballast, Matt's 11 lb Bruce stern-bucket method, Fisherman storm anchor, Little Cruiser 9 lb Danforth + 25 lb Luke below, bow-roller trim problem), [fastening and gluing](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md) (Matt considers ring nails structural — joint can fail at the next lamination), [Matt Layden](/matt-layden/biography.md) and [build effort](/construction/build-effort.md) (original built in ~4 months full-time, bicycle-trailer haul, ravine launch to the Salmon River), [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md) (started building "Walden" in Brisbane from 9 mm ply, 1:10 scale model), and [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md). Promoted the interior detail, the Exuma Sound performance point, and the tough/simple original-build story into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md); populated the design/people/references indexes.
* **Update (batch page_031–page_040)**: Added detail from the July 2001 "weight of Paradox", "Started layout of ply", "Anchors", and "Lugsail" threads. Created design concepts [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (≈1,365 lb loaded; ≈930 lb in the bottom 10 in incl. 400 lb lead as 10×40 lb pigs; ~1 in per 200 lb waterplane) and [rig and sail](/design/rig.md) (lug-type sail, yard held only by the halyard/no parrel, rolling boom); construction concepts [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md) (bulkhead-#2/transom/side-panel fits and fixes) and [non-skid deck finish](/construction/non-skid-deck.md) (Glen's walnut-shells-in-epoxy method); operations concept [ground tackle and anchoring](/operations/ground-tackle.md); person [Bert Vercauteren](/people/bert-vercauteren.md) (Belgium; bought plans as inspiration for a two-person micro cruiser); and reference [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) (Dave & Mindy Bolduc's similar boat, cruised two-up). Promoted weight/ballast figures and the lug-rig description into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md). Extended [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md), and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md); populated the design/construction/operations/people/references indexes.
* **Update (batch page_021–page_030)**: Added detail from the July 2001 "Getting the bottom on" / "More questions" / plans-and-cost threads. Created design concept [mast partners and vent trunk](/design/mast-partners.md) (the reinforced deck area around the mast; control lines led into the cabin; Glen built a lower-mast mock-up before decking); reference [building plans (source and cost)](/references/building-plans.md) (Dave Bolduc, Greensboro NC, $35 US); and person [Paul (NZ)](/people/paul-nz.md) (prospective builder drawn to enclosed, simple-rig sailing). Extended [hull scantlings](/design/hull-scantlings.md) with the full plywood bill of materials (1×6, 3×9, 4×12, 2×18 mm — 10 sheets plus solid timber), [fastening and gluing](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md) with nail-sizing/pre-installation guidance, and [Alastair](/people/alastair.md). Promoted rig/mast-partners, heavy-displacement chop handling, and the plans source into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md). Populated the design/people/references indexes.
* **Update (batch page_011–page_020)**: Added construction/finishing knowledge from the July 2001 "Bottom Paint" and "Getting the bottom on" threads. Created design concept [hull scantlings](/design/hull-scantlings.md) (18 mm bottom / 12 mm sides ply); construction concepts [hull planking](/construction/hull-planking.md), [fastening and gluing](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md) (plans' bronze ring nails at 40–70 mm vs. Glen's removable screws; Cab-O-Sil to resist squeeze-out), and [bottom painting over epoxy](/construction/bottom-painting.md) (bond antifouling to a tacking-off epoxy sealer coat); people [Chuck Leinweber](/people/chuck-leinweber.md) and [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md); reference [Duckworks Magazine](/references/duckworks-magazine.md). Extended [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (Gold Coast Yachts background, techniques), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) (follow-the-plans stance), [Alastair](/people/alastair.md), and the [mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md) (construction/stove file folders). Promoted hull scantlings, plywood-and-epoxy construction, and a cabin stove into the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md), and populated the design index.
* **Update (batch page_001–page_010)**: Added community and early-builder knowledge from the first ten archived emails. Created people concepts [Matt Layden](/matt-layden/biography.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md), [Chris Curtis](/people/chris-curtis.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair.md); references [mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md), [Building the Paradox articles](/references/building-the-paradox-articles.md), [Study Plans](/references/study-plans.md); construction [build effort and workspace](/construction/build-effort.md); operations [Texas 200](/operations/texas-200.md). Updated the trunk [Paradox](/paradox.md) with community/mailing-list history and a capability summary, and populated the people/references/construction/operations indexes. Key facts: group history (Yahoo→groups.io), ~500 hrs / 2-year build, buildable in a garage ~2 ft longer than the boat, and a Paradox completing the Texas 200.
* **Initialization**: Created the Paradox Sailboat OKF bundle — root [index](/index.md), trunk concept [Paradox](/paradox.md), and seed subdirectories `design/`, `construction/`, `people/`, `operations/`, `references/`. Knowledge is sourced incrementally from the `source/` email archive (10,065 messages).
